what: TREE-FRAME CUDA GRAPHS ring A/B (gate 3, perf/tree-frame-graphs) — the perstream-trees-ab arms re-run with tree graphs ARMED and capture-PROVEN on every stage ('tree capture OK' in stage logs, npad=24). VERDICT: trees remain NET-NEGATIVE per-stream on this ring class (-22..-61% at B=4, medians of 3), with the mechanism now nailed three layers deep: (1) COMPUTE — FIXED: the 07-12 kill reason (154ms eager tree stage compute vs 45ms graphed chains) is gone; graphs captured and replayed live on all stages (bit-exact per the on-box receipt). (2) BYTES — chains yes, trees no: an M25_FP8_AUX=1 pass (halved aux) lifted CHAIN arms +20..+125%/stream (code 13.2->20.3, mix-B8 5.2->11.6 — a real standalone win, g held, receipts valid) but moved tree rounds NOT AT ALL. (3) SYNC-LATENCY — the binding term: tree rounds cost a flat ~207ms on this ring regardless of content, batch, aux size, or accept — the naked ring traversal, paid because a tree round drains the pipeline (dirty-frontier contract) while chain rounds hide RTT behind de-lockstep + cwnd keep-warm (60-87ms effective). The g lever itself reproduced: +13..+76% committed/round (summarize +75%, prose +30%, tools +26%, mix +22%, qa +17%, reasoning +13%; code -3% — chains already match code). NEXT LEVER (scoped, not built): pipeline tree rounds across streams / async tree rounds — a coordinator-scheduling problem; tail-side tree_greedy_walk aux slimming remains worthwhile for the return leg generally. KILL CRITERION: stands for THIS ring class at the current sync design; the 07-12 solo +18% tree win (sync cost amortized over one stream at depth-1) is unaffected.
ring: 6x RTX5090 EU (DE head [0:10), NO [10:22), FI [22:34), BG [34:46), IT [46:58), IT tail [58:62)), iids 45640267/45640272/45640296/45640937/45640939/45640292, label treegraph-ab, M25_BATCH=8 kv 4096 K=8 max_new 96, fp8 wire, aux_local ON, graphs on (M25_GRAPH_MAX=32, tree budget 4), M25_TREE_M=12 TOPB=3 DEPTH=8, receipts ON all passes (6/6 valid every arm). Return leg: head->tail via FSN relay circuit, DCUtR-upgraded to DIRECT (a consumer-ISP peering hole made the tail's addr unreachable from the head only — SHARD_RET_MADDR override, committed this branch; the relay-rendezvous design validated live)
method: SWEEP_TREE_ARMS=off,on x3 reps (medians) + capture-proven discriminator pass + SWEEP_K=6 chain reference (partially degraded, k8 reference used — trees lose vs k8 a fortiori) + M25_FP8_AUX=1 off,on pass
spend: ~$37 total across 2 days (incl. 3 aborted provisioning cycles: heavy-image stall, vast ssh-proxy aliasing two iids, dead-man switch firing on a stale heartbeat mid-warm — all three now have committed fixes/process guards)
branch: perf/tree-frame-graphs (PR #138); launcher fixes f1d3bbd (SHARD_SSH_OVERRIDE) + circuit return (SHARD_RET_MADDR); capture log for arm proof
logs: session scratchpad sweep_rep{1,2,3}.log sweep_disc.log sweep_fp8aux.log sweep_k6.log + ab_table.txt
